2606.13673.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
2606.13673.paper_summary: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
2606.13673.paper_summary_zh: 空間推理，即確定物體位置、它們之間的關係以及它們在三維空間中的運動的能力，對於視覺-語言模型（VLMs）來說仍然是一個根本挑戰。工具增強代理試圖通過用專業的感知模塊增強VLM來解決這個問題，但它們的有效性受到通過這些工具調用的行動介面的限制。在這項工作中，我們研究這個介面的設計如何塑造代理進行開放式空間推理的能力。現有的空間代理要麼使用單次執行代碼，這在觀察到任何中間結果之前就承諾了完整的分析策略，要麼依賴於結構化的工具調用介面，這通常對自由組合操作或根據每個任務量身定制分析提供了較少的靈活性。這兩種設計對於開放式的複雜3D/4D空間推理提供的靈活性有限。因此，我們提出了SpatialClaw，一個無需訓練的空間推理框架，採用代碼作為行動介面。SpatialClaw維持一個狀態保持的Python內核，預加載輸入幀和一套感知及幾何原語，讓一個基於VLM的代理在每一步根據所有先前的輸出寫出一個可執行單元，從而使代理能夠靈活地組合和操作感知結果，並根據中間文本和視覺觀察以及每個問題的需求調整其分析。在涵蓋廣泛靜態和動態3D/4D空間推理任務的20個空間推理基準上進行評估，SpatialClaw實現了59.9%的平均準確率，超越了最近的空間代理11.2個百分點，並在來自兩個模型系列的六個VLM骨幹上持續獲得增益，無需任何基準或模型特定的調整。
2606.13673.author: Seokju Cho et.al.
2606.13673.authors: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen
